title: Understanding Angular Velocity
text: Angular velocity is the rate of change of angular displacement and is usually measured in radians per second $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.

title: Given Formula for Angular Velocity
text: The problem states that the angular velocity is given by the formula $$omega=25t$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, where $$t$$ is the time in seconds.

title: Substitute the Given Time
text: To find the instantaneous angular velocity at $$t=2.0$$ s, substitute $$t=2.0$$ into the formula $$omega=25t$$.

title: Calculate the Angular Velocity
text: Substitute $$t=2.0$$ into the formula $$omega=25t$$ to find the angular velocity.
